title: The Works of Edgar Allan Poe — Volume 2
author: Poe, Edgar Allan
summary: In Paris one gusty autumn evening the narrator and C. Auguste Dupin entertain the Prefect of Police, Monsieur G., who seeks Dupin's aid in recovering a purloined letter stolen from a royal lady by Minister D. The minister had cleverly exchanged his own note to seize the incriminating paper while it lay address-up on a table. Though the police have repeatedly ransacked the minister’s Hotel, inspecting furniture, rungs, mirrors, carpets, and adjoining houses with microscopes and needles, and even waylaid his person, the letter eludes detection. Dupin hints that the very simplicity of the concealment may be the cause, indeed.
